nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Warren of Wissington was born in 1485, in Nayland, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Warren, was 25 and his mother, Eleanor Gerard, was 27. He had at least 1 son with Margaret Cornwallis. He died on 22 February 1544, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Warren of Wissington est né en 1485, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Warren, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Gerard, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret Cornwallis. Il est décédé le 22 février 1544, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Warren of Wissington 1485 in Nayland, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Warren 25 und seine Mutter Eleanor Gerard 27. Er hatte mit Margaret Cornwallis mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 22. Februar 1544 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Warren of Wissington è nato il nel 1485 nel Nayland, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Warren, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Gerard, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margaret Cornwallis. Morì il 22 febbraio 1544, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.
